The '''FastTurn-3 Hydrospanner''' was a model of [[Hydrospanner/Legends|hydrospanner]] that was manufactured by [[Regallis Engineering/Legends|Regallis Engineering]].

==Specifications==
The FastTurn-3 was a model of [[Hydrospanner/Legends|hydrospanner]] manufactured by [[Regallis Engineering/Legends|Regallis Engineering]].<ref name="EGWT" /> It employed a hydraulic compression cylinder that mechanically<ref name="NEGWT">''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref> increased the tool's torque, allowing the user to fasten bolts without exerting themselves too much. The socket was hinged, and able to rotate one hundred and eighty degrees in any direction, allowing the user to get the tool into tight spaces and angles. The socket itself was capable of fitting nuts and fasteners as small as two [[Millimeter/Legends|millimeters]],<ref name="EGWT" /> with special attachments allowing the tool to fit over larger size fasteners, such as [[fusion bolt]]s which could be up to twenty-five [[Centimeter/Legends|centimeters]] in diameter.<ref name="NEGWT" />

Standard drive bits could be inserted into the FastTurn-3's rotary driver, and the torque generated by the tool was capable of driving screws and bolts securely into materials [[Duraplast/Legends|duraplast]] and [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]]. A universal drive bit could fit around any screw, bolt, or fastener by using seventy retracting [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] pins. A tiny [[Fusioncutter/Legends|fusioncutter]] was attached to the hydrospanner,<ref name="EGWT" /> and was used to cut around bolts,<ref name="NEGWT" /> removing molecular adhesives and fusion welds prior to the removal of the bolts.<ref name="EGWT" />

A thirty centimeter long extension cable, which accepted standard drive bits and sockets, could be fitted to the FastTurn-3 for working inside machinery. The cable was capable of being threaded around obstructions, and was fitted with a vid receptor line that relayed a real-time video feed to a connected [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], allowing the user to guide it through tight confines and operate it.<ref name="EGWT" />

Space drive bits could be stored in the shaft of the FastTurn-3, accessed through a panel at the bottom of the tool. Next to the access panel was a [[Dataport/Legends|dataport]]. Internal [[Power cell/Legends|power cells]] were located just behind the hydraulic compression cylinder.<ref name="EGWT" />
